title: Stelis trulla
taxonomy: Stelis trulla is classified within the plant family Orchidaceae. This family comprises over 30,000 species and is one of the largest and most diverse in the plant kingdom. Stelis trulla is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is a group of orchids characterized by their long, slender stems and showy flowers. Stelis trulla is a member of the tribe Epidendreae within the subfamily Epidendroideae.

intro: Stelis trulla, also known as "Trulla" or "Stelis", is a flowering plant species belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. Stelis trulla contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis trulla is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems.
